item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much to everyone who upvoted!

Professor Faymaren sends you her best wishes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
kenowhere

I'm admittedly not sure what this means. I googled the word and it's part of a hub related to GAW? Looks interesting—even if unintentional, thanks for the new reading material!

I'm glad you liked the piece! I had a lot of help from my reviewers with getting the tone polished up. Thanks for the comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
happy magic kenowhere

nah but seriously this is a really nice idea, and I like how happy the tone stays throughout while still being clinical. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the kind comment! I'm glad you liked the idea. :)

I've been wondering about a desire path skip for a while

You can definitely still write a desire path skip! Just needs to be distinct from the current material (for example, we've got a bunch of articles about anomalous violins, and they each do well in their own niche).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fairy godmother… Faymaren… I just got that. Nice! I've been wondering about a desire path skip for a while, and this did it far better than anything I had in mind. Just a lovely idea well captured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the +1!

Potential spoilers
hide

Fairy godmothers know that dogs are goodest bois and deserve happy endings too.

I originally added the dog in as a nod to some retellings of fairy tales (I read a lot of picture books at the local library as a kid, lol.) that specifically went out of their way to mention that the protagonist's animals didn't get forgotten. Like Cinderella's dead/missing dad's horse getting to live in the royal stables, or a helpful cat in… Persian Cinderella? Wild West Cinderella? I forget… getting to live it up in a castle or the like.

In early drafts I just wrote that the dog made feathers appear too. Reviewers liked the dog and told me to add more to the test results, so I did.

Also one of my best friends who could also use a fairy godmother (we all could, really) has a very good boy who is basically her therapy animal and he deserves good things too he is a good boi.

Happy (upcoming) New Year to you and your doggos!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhhhhhh!! That explains so much!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding the dog in there got me… it was fairly average until the end, but then the dog happened and got treats and dammit that reminded me of my rescue dogs and now I need to go get them a bunch of treats and more toys… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you Conwell! I owe a lot to my reviewers; they helped me a lot with the trimming and editing to make sure the writing didn't drag. I'm glad you enjoyed the piece!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you liked the read! The MTF agent was something I edited in after recommendations of multiple reviewers—it's thanks to them that the guy exists as he does and has a nice thing happen to him.

I agree that it's rare to see nice things happen in the Foundation-verse; I feel like there needs to be some balance in there to keep people motivated to show up for work every day.

Thanks for the comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the nice comment and the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do wonder why there are several of these, instead of just one singular path.

potential spoilers
hide

As there are numerous fairy tales, there are multiple fairy godmothers out there. Each has her own territory and her own set of handpicked clients (but pretty much all of them just are called "fairy godmother").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As always, your skips manage to capture a sense of wonder and whimsy in so few words. A fun read that doesn't overstay its welcome or get bogged down in magic systems and deeper lore than is needed.

+1. A thoroughly enjoyable SCP, Zyn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a nice and wholesome article. I especially like the part about the retired/injured MTF Agent and his dog. These guys rarely have something nice happen to them. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's… beautiful. Such a simple article, but full of emotions. Zyn, you've done it again.

+100

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, I do wonder why there are several of these, instead of just one singular path. I feel it would have more of a "magical feel" if it was just the pqth youd have to find. This is very wubjrctive and minor though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I correct in assuming that the Foundation is kinda using the anomaly to benefit its employees under the guise of "research"?

There's no "guise", it's totally scientific research with screened test subjects and results that contribute significantly to better understanding of the anomaly. The additional effects are just a welcome extra benefit. ;)

Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A nice uplifting SCP! Am I correct in assuming that the Foundation is kinda using the anomaly to benefit its employees under the guise of "research"? The final note there at the end seems to suggest it.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the nice comment! I'm glad you liked the read! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only skimmed this and yet, I still knew in my head that this was most likely made by Zyn. You have a really good, distinct writing style, I love it!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the review! I agree with your statement about the clinical language; I've made some fixes per your specifications. The date was more of a placeholder than anything, but I've input some numbers since it's not particularly necessary for the date to be blackboxed. I appreciate your upvote!

Show spoilers?
spoilers!

'anomalies being nice to people for no specific reasons'

The pathways are sort of the modern version of appealing to a fairy godmother for some help/a little grandmotherly support.

Seeing these days people don't really have access a phone book where they can invite fairies to a child's birthday and local witches/wise women aren't really a thing, Foundation-verse fairy godmothers (or at least some of them) are in need of deserving people to help out. This is how they get in touch with those people.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

Aww, this one made me feel good. It's wholesome without being saccharine and has an interesting, unique concept. There's only a few things that I'd pick up on as things I wouldn't personally do if this was written by me; however, I understand some of this may be personal taste.

  • I feel that non-clinical language in quotes is overused a little here, with the worst offenders for me being the "desire path" in the image caption and the "feathers effect". For the former, I think the phrase can be completely removed, as reading the article shows us that the image must in fact be a desire path. For the latter, something along the lines of 'SCP-4722's primary anomalous effect' may be better, but that could need a little more reworking if you choose to change it.
  • I'm unsure about the blackboxing of the date. I get it for the names in this case, but unless my lore knowledge is lacking the date should be fine?
  • This is definitely the most personal of the three, but I wouldn't use phrases like 'soon' and 'a few minutes' this much in the description. I feel the Foundation would at least try to assign some numbers or ranges to a few of these.

These are all fairly minor qualms, though, and at no point did I consider not upvoting this because of them. While 'anomalies being nice to people for no specific reasons' may be wearing a little bit thin, there's still some space for them, especially when they're this unique and nice to read.


Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to hear that! I tried to incorporate your feedback as best I could while keeping the other reviewers in mind. Thanks again for taking a look at this in PMs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like the little bit about the professor you added. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, thank you so much! I really appreciate that you were willing to read it twice, especially since the first time was at a weird hour of the night. Much obliged!

ngl now i want curly fries…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was such a pleasant skip to read! The 740 words you were able to fit it under made it crisp and sweet (like a curly fry!) I think this was really fun to read, and thank you so much for letting me read it! You have my 1+ and my best wishes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, same.

Thanks again for the review!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IKR?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ya know, I could really use one of these bad boys right about now

item.title: Author Post!
item.content:encoded:
Naming this was a decision between "Paths to Desire" and "Paths to Fulfillment". I ended up going with the latter. Thanks to Calibri Bold and RockTeethMothEyes, respectively, for the suggestions!

So, big, big thanks to all my reviewers in chat: asaucedev, Kilonova, Jak/JakdragonX (x2), RockTeethMothEyes, LuciusIII, MalyceGraves, Calibri_Bold, TheMightyMcB

Particular thanks to asaucedev and TheMightyMcB for line-by-line specifics, and Jak for reading this twice!

Cerastes reviewed this in PMs! Thank you!

Image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Diagonal_path,_Recreation_Ground,_Burley_-_geograph.org.uk_-_263086.jpg

Spoilers
hide

So… very bluntly, this is the modern version of how you get yourself the help of a fairy godmother. Obviously though, since these days it's kind of weird to water a stick with your tears while sitting at your mom's grave, all you gotta do is run along a pathway that defies human infrastructure.

Nearly all of the specific quirks (the clothes turning glowy, the feathers, dropped items staying conveniently in place - can you imagine if Prince Charming had to look for a shoe in the dark of midnight?) are nods to fairy tales.

Also, "Marraine" translates from French to "godmother".

This is something that's been sitting in my "document of things I want to write someday" for months. It's been awhile since I wrote something; I figured at the end of the year's a good a time as any. Here's hoping that everyone out there has someone/finds someone who will cheer them on through life, and best wishes for the upcoming new year!
